publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 166, folios 228-229. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: St James' Sqre 4 o'clock Tuesday Afternoon

I lose no time in letting you know, Dearest Mate, that (to my infinite annoyance) I am obliged to remain in Town till Friday certainly; & possibly till Satdy; on account of my teeth. Lauzelle says he cannot possibly finish before Thursdy at the earliest, & that it is of great importance; for he has found ample mischief as the
cause of the pain I have suffered.

I have written to let Harmes & the Coach-man know for Friday, when I do hope we may go. I suppose the man at Bridgwater will have the sense to stop for us.-- I cannot write to him, for I don't know how to address him.
It's a deuce of a plague; but it can't be helped.-- Don't be angry with the poor Avis, my dear Exiguus Mus.
